title: Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs
summary: Allen Pike discusses voice-in, visuals-out as a paradigm for LLM-powered products, arguing it represents optimal AI user experience. The talk covers techniques for achieving low-latency responsive AI agents and design lessons from building such products at Forestwalk Labs.
writeup: Allen Pike of Forestwalk Labs presents "voice in, visuals out" as a design paradigm for LLM-powered products, arguing that pairing spoken input with visual output makes for an optimal AI user experience. The talk covers techniques for building low-latency, responsive AI agents and shares design lessons drawn from building such products at Forestwalk Labs. The takeaway is a concrete framing for product design, where voice serves as input and visuals carry the response, alongside the latency work needed to make agents feel responsive.
